article_title: ESA and JAXA Sign RAMSES Cooperation Agreement to Send Spacecraft to Asteroid Apophis Ahead of 2029 Earth Flyby
summary: ESA-JAXA Ramses cooperation agreement signed in Berlin on 7 May — every claim, quote and trajectory detail verified against all 5 snapshots (4 ESA primary sources + Phys.org via Archive.org fallback).

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

content_preview.title: ESA and JAXA Sign RAMSES Cooperation Agreement to Send Spacecraft to Asteroid Apophis Ahead of 2029 Earth Flyby
content_preview.summary: Berlin signing on 7 May commits Japan to launch ESA's Ramses probe on an H3 rocket in 2028, in time to escort Apophis through its 32,000 km flyby of Earth.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The European Space Agency and the Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency have signed a formal cooperation agreement for the Ramses mission to near-Earth asteroid Apophis, locking in Japan's role on a probe that must launch in 2028 to reach the asteroid before its close flyby of Earth in April 2029. The signing took place on 7 May at the Embassy of Italy in Berlin, where [ESA Director General Josef Aschbacher and JAXA President Hiroshi Yamakawa](https://www.esa.int/Space_Safety/Planetary...

editor_notes.content_quality: Tight News piece (660 words) with focused structure: Overview / What the Agreement Covers / Why Apophis and Why Now / What JAXA Brings / What We Don't Know / Context. Inline citations on every numeric and quoted claim.
editor_notes.source_verification: 5 sources, all verified. source-0 (ESA 7 May announcement on planetary defence): confirms Aschbacher/Yamakawa Berlin signing, Embassy of Italy venue, JAXA solar arrays + infrared imager + H3 launch, Apophis 13 April 2029 / 32,000 km / one-tenth-Moon-distance / closer-than-geosynchronous-orbits, BepiColombo/EarthCARE/Hera lineage. source-1 (ESA Ramses mission page): confirms 375 m diameter, 13 April 2029, April 2028 launch, February 2029 arrival, Paolo Martino quote 'truly feel like one globally integrated team' verbatim, before-and-after measurements. source-2 (ESA earlier potential-collaboration update): confirms Holger Krag 'ESA welcomes JAXA's increasing interest in participating in the Ramses mission. International collaboration lies at the heart of planetary defence' verbatim, Hera-as-first-planetary-defence-mission language. source-3 (Phys.org via Archive.org fallback): confirms Berlin signing, Aschbacher/Yamakawa, 32,000 km, accompany-through-flyby framing — but original phys.org was bot-blocked so the fallback is the canonical record. source-4 (ESA-JAXA Tsukuba joint statement Nov 2024): confirms Ramses is one of 'four areas' for expanded cooperation, names thermal infrared imager, solar array wings, launch.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every numeric detail (375 m, 32,000 km, 13 April 2029, April 2028 launch, February 2029 arrival), every name (Aschbacher, Yamakawa, Krag, Martino), every venue (Embassy of Italy in Berlin), every direct quote (Krag, Martino), and the mission lineage (Hera, BepiColombo, EarthCARE) trace to verified ESA primary sources. Headline, summary, and Overview lead are all anchored in source-0.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, professional, no editorializing. The What-We-Don't-Know section appropriately scopes the open questions (launch date, full instrument complement, total cost, member-state funding breakdown).
editor_notes.originality: No prior Apophis/Ramses/JAXA/ESA-planetary-defence coverage in 2026-05 (Galaxeye, IcarusRaven are different satellite-side stories). Genuinely new beat.
recommendations: phys.org Archive.org fallback worked cleanly here — keep as-is. ESA is already a reputable primary source domain.

source_verification_per_url.notes: ESA primary announcement of the 7 May Berlin signing. Confirms Aschbacher / Yamakawa / Embassy of Italy / Berlin venue, JAXA solar arrays + infrared imager + H3 launch, 13 April 2029 / 32,000 km / one-tenth-Moon-distance / closer-than-geosynchronous-orbits, lineage list.
ESA mission dedicated page. Confirms 375 m diameter, April 2028 launch / February 2029 arrival schedule, Paolo Martino 'one globally integrated team' quote verbatim, before-and-after survey framing.

ESA earlier update on the negotiation. Confirms Holger Krag quote verbatim ('ESA welcomes JAXA's increasing interest in participating in the Ramses mission. International collaboration lies at the heart of planetary defence'), Hera-as-first-planetary-defence-mission framing, JAXA already participating in Hera.
Archive.org fallback (live HTTP 200 but bot-blocked). Confirms Aschbacher/Yamakawa Berlin signing, accompany-throughout-flyby framing, 32,000 km figure.
Nov 2024 Tsukuba joint statement. Confirms Ramses among four areas for expanded cooperation, names thermal infrared imager / solar array wings / launch opportunities.

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean APPROVE. Tightly sourced primary-source story on ESA-JAXA planetary defence cooperation with verified quotes, dates, and orbital parameters.
